# Responses Reference

Most handlers do not need to create response objects. Return plain Python
values when that is enough; use explicit response classes when you need
status codes, headers, streaming, redirects, or a specific content type.

```python
from quater import JSONResponse, RedirectResponse, StreamResponse
```

## Automatic Return Values

| Handler returns | Quater sends |
| --- | --- |
| `dict`, `list`, dataclass, `msgspec.Struct` | JSON response |
| `str` | UTF-8 text response |
| `bytes`, `bytearray`, `memoryview` | Byte response |
| `None` | Empty `204` response |
| [`Response`](#symbol-response) instance | Sent as-is |

## Response Classes

## Response {#symbol-response}

Public import: `from quater import Response`.

Use this when you already have bytes and want full control.

```python
Response(
    body: bytes = b'',
    *,
    status_code: int = 200,
    headers: HeaderItems | Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
    content_type: str | None = None,
)
```

### Parameters

| Name | Type | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `body` | `bytes` | Raw response body. |
| `status_code` | `int` | HTTP status code. |
| `headers` | [`HeaderItems`](/en/latest/reference/request#type-headeritems) \| Mapping[str, str] \| None | Response headers. |
| `content_type` | `str \| None` | Sets `content-type` when not already set. |

## JSONResponse {#symbol-jsonresponse}

Public import: `from quater import JSONResponse`.

Use this when you need explicit status or headers for JSON.

```python
JSONResponse(
    content: object,
    *,
    status_code: int = 200,
    headers: HeaderItems | Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
)
```

### Parameters

| Name | Type | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `content` | `object` | Value serialized as JSON with msgspec. |
| `status_code` | `int` | HTTP status code. |
| `headers` | [`HeaderItems`](/en/latest/reference/request#type-headeritems) \| Mapping[str, str] \| None | Response headers. |

## TextResponse {#symbol-textresponse}

Public import: `from quater import TextResponse`.

Use this for plain text.

```python
TextResponse(
    content: str,
    *,
    status_code: int = 200,
    headers: HeaderItems | Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
    content_type: str = 'text/plain; charset=utf-8',
)
```

### Parameters

| Name | Type | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `content` | `str` | Text encoded as UTF-8. |
| `status_code` | `int` | HTTP status code. |
| `headers` | [`HeaderItems`](/en/latest/reference/request#type-headeritems) \| Mapping[str, str] \| None | Response headers. |
| `content_type` | `str` | Text content type. |

## HTMLResponse {#symbol-htmlresponse}

Public import: `from quater import HTMLResponse`.

Use this for HTML.

```python
HTMLResponse(
    content: str,
    *,
    status_code: int = 200,
    headers: HeaderItems | Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
)
```

### Parameters

| Name | Type | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `content` | `str` | HTML encoded as UTF-8. |
| `status_code` | `int` | HTTP status code. |
| `headers` | [`HeaderItems`](/en/latest/reference/request#type-headeritems) \| Mapping[str, str] \| None | Response headers. |

## BytesResponse {#symbol-bytesresponse}

Public import: `from quater import BytesResponse`.

Use this for raw bytes.

```python
BytesResponse(
    content: ResponseBody,
    *,
    status_code: int = 200,
    headers: HeaderItems | Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
    content_type: str = 'application/octet-stream',
)
```

### Parameters

| Name | Type | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `content` | [`ResponseBody`](/en/latest/reference/responses#type-responsebody) | Bytes-like body value. |
| `status_code` | `int` | HTTP status code. |
| `headers` | [`HeaderItems`](/en/latest/reference/request#type-headeritems) \| Mapping[str, str] \| None | Response headers. |
| `content_type` | `str` | Byte response content type. |

## StreamResponse {#symbol-streamresponse}

Public import: `from quater import StreamResponse`.

Use this for async byte streams.

```python
StreamResponse(
    body_iterator: AsyncIterable[bytes],
    *,
    status_code: int = 200,
    headers: HeaderItems | Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
    content_type: str = 'application/octet-stream',
)
```

### Parameters

| Name | Type | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `body_iterator` | `AsyncIterable[bytes]` | Async iterator yielding bytes. |
| `status_code` | `int` | HTTP status code. |
| `headers` | [`HeaderItems`](/en/latest/reference/request#type-headeritems) \| Mapping[str, str] \| None | Response headers. |
| `content_type` | `str` | Stream content type. |

## RedirectResponse {#symbol-redirectresponse}

Public import: `from quater import RedirectResponse`.

Use this for redirects.

```python
RedirectResponse(
    location: str,
    *,
    status_code: int = 307,
    headers: HeaderItems | Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
)
```

### Parameters

| Name | Type | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `location` | `str` | Redirect target. |
| `status_code` | `int` | Redirect status code. |
| `headers` | [`HeaderItems`](/en/latest/reference/request#type-headeritems) \| Mapping[str, str] \| None | Response headers. |

## EmptyResponse {#symbol-emptyresponse}

Public import: `from quater import EmptyResponse`.

Use this for responses with no body.

```python
EmptyResponse(
    *,
    status_code: int = 204,
    headers: HeaderItems | Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
)
```

### Parameters

| Name | Type | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `status_code` | `int` | HTTP status code. |
| `headers` | [`HeaderItems`](/en/latest/reference/request#type-headeritems) \| Mapping[str, str] \| None | Response headers. |

## Type Names Used Here

| Name | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| <span id="type-headeritems"></span>`HeaderItems` | `(name, value)` header pairs. See [Request headers](./request#headers). |
| <span id="type-responsebody"></span>`ResponseBody` | Bytes-like value accepted by [`BytesResponse`](#symbol-bytesresponse). |
| <span id="type-asynciterable-bytes"></span>`AsyncIterable[bytes]` | Async stream of response chunks. |
